schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: a7e55ad7e91313f9329bca994811c654e73b818a
findings:
  - { id: judged-dcj-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 and dc-3 adopt the ADJ-28 'completion reading' that narrows parent dc-5's literal trigger — 'a stub card with no matching active story checks refs/heads/design/\u003cstub-slug\u003e' — to fire only when NO matching spec resolves anywhere (neither active nor archived). Under parent dc-5's frozen text, a stub whose only implementing story sits in specs/archive/ has no matching ACTIVE story, so the ref-check runs and (ref present) the 'instantiated on design/\u003cslug\u003e, not yet in this checkout's active store' notice renders; under this story's dc-1/dc-3, that same stub instead renders an archived-disclosure board link and the notice is explicitly forbidden. That is a behavioral contradiction of parent dc-5's stated firing condition, and the story itself records that it is resolved by interpretation only — 'no supersedes/exempts edge, no ratification, no supersession machinery.' If ADJ-28 adjudication does not substitute for a declared supersedes/exempts edge against spec/workbench-legibility dc-5 in the decision graph, this is an undeclared conflict: the reinterpretation renders dc-5's literal sentence false in the archived-match case while leaving the graph showing full inheritance. (confidence 0.50)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "ADJ-28 (round6-adjudications.ndjson) is the controller's authoritative adjudication of this sweep's cf-1/cf-2 tension: it fixes the completion reading and expressly declines any supersedes/exempts edge or parent ratification (ADJ-26 forbids such an edge this round), choosing interpretation. The finding's own conditional — a conflict only 'if ADJ-28 adjudication does not substitute for a declared edge' — is answered: ADJ-28 IS that substitution, recorded non-silently. Under it the alleged conflict is a false positive (03 §Decision-conflict gate's no-conflict value): parent ac-2's active-match link is a SUFFICIENT, not exclusive, condition, so linking an archived match contradicts no frozen ac-2 text; and parent dc-5's 'no matching active story' trigger is scoped to the in-between state it was settling — the archived case being the gap dc-5's model omitted, not a case dc-5 governs — so every frozen sentence stays true while co-2 keeps the archived render honest. Mapping: no-conflict (false-positive-with-note), NOT rejected (the spec is not realigned — the completion reading stands) and NOT superseded/exempt (ADJ-28 declines all supersession machinery). Hand-dispositioned as the sanctioned last-of-its-kind flow; spec/disposition-verb (Wave B) will own this write henceforth.", target_ref: spec/workbench-legibility }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dcj-1\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 and dc-3 adopt the ADJ-28 'completion reading' that narrows parent dc-5's literal trigger — 'a stub card with no matching active story checks refs/heads/design/\u003cstub-slug\u003e' — to fire only when NO matching spec resolves anywhere (neither active nor archived). Under parent dc-5's frozen text, a stub whose only implementing story sits in specs/archive/ has no matching ACTIVE story, so the ref-check runs and (ref present) the 'instantiated on design/\u003cslug\u003e, not yet in this checkout's active store' notice renders; under this story's dc-1/dc-3, that same stub instead renders an archived-disclosure board link and the notice is explicitly forbidden. That is a behavioral contradiction of parent dc-5's stated firing condition, and the story itself records that it is resolved by interpretation only — 'no supersedes/exempts edge, no ratification, no supersession machinery.' If ADJ-28 adjudication does not substitute for a declared supersedes/exempts edge against spec/workbench-legibility dc-5 in the decision graph, this is an undeclared conflict: the reinterpretation renders dc-5's literal sentence false in the archived-match case while leaving the graph showing full inheritance.\",\"confidence\":0.5,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-legibility\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dcj-1** [no-conflict]: Story dc-1 and dc-3 adopt the ADJ-28 'completion reading' that narrows parent dc-5's literal trigger — 'a stub card with no matching active story checks refs/heads/design/<stub-slug>' — to fire only when NO matching spec resolves anywhere (neither active nor archived). Under parent dc-5's frozen text, a stub whose only implementing story sits in specs/archive/ has no matching ACTIVE story, so the ref-check runs and (ref present) the 'instantiated on design/<slug>, not yet in this checkout's active store' notice renders; under this story's dc-1/dc-3, that same stub instead renders an archived-disclosure board link and the notice is explicitly forbidden. That is a behavioral contradiction of parent dc-5's stated firing condition, and the story itself records that it is resolved by interpretation only — 'no supersedes/exempts edge, no ratification, no supersession machinery.' If ADJ-28 adjudication does not substitute for a declared supersedes/exempts edge against spec/workbench-legibility dc-5 in the decision graph, this is an undeclared conflict: the reinterpretation renders dc-5's literal sentence false in the archived-match case while leaving the graph showing full inheritance. (confidence 0.50) — ADJ-28 (round6-adjudications.ndjson) is the controller's authoritative adjudication of this sweep's cf-1/cf-2 tension: it fixes the completion reading and expressly declines any supersedes/exempts edge or parent ratification (ADJ-26 forbids such an edge this round), choosing interpretation. The finding's own conditional — a conflict only 'if ADJ-28 adjudication does not substitute for a declared edge' — is answered: ADJ-28 IS that substitution, recorded non-silently. Under it the alleged conflict is a false positive (03 §Decision-conflict gate's no-conflict value): parent ac-2's active-match link is a SUFFICIENT, not exclusive, condition, so linking an archived match contradicts no frozen ac-2 text; and parent dc-5's 'no matching active story' trigger is scoped to the in-between state it was settling — the archived case being the gap dc-5's model omitted, not a case dc-5 governs — so every frozen sentence stays true while co-2 keeps the archived render honest. Mapping: no-conflict (false-positive-with-note), NOT rejected (the spec is not realigned — the completion reading stands) and NOT superseded/exempt (ADJ-28 declines all supersession machinery). Hand-dispositioned as the sanctioned last-of-its-kind flow; spec/disposition-verb (Wave B) will own this write henceforth.
